findings.title: Stored XSS in customer statements export
findings.description: User-supplied memo fields are persisted without output encoding and rendered verbatim in exported HTML statements, allowing stored cross-site scripting against back-office users.

findings.title: Hardcoded API credentials in payments service
findings.description: Static API credentials are committed in the payments service configuration, exposing downstream payment rails if the repository or image is leaked.

findings.title: SQL injection in legacy reporting module
findings.description: A legacy reporting endpoint concatenates user input directly into SQL, permitting extraction or modification of report data.

findings.title: Missing MFA on internal admin console
findings.description: The internal admin console accepts single-factor password login for privileged operators, leaving high-privilege accounts exposed to credential stuffing and phishing.

findings.title: Unrestricted file upload type on document intake
findings.description: The document intake endpoint does not restrict uploaded file types, allowing potentially executable or malicious content to be stored and served.

findings.title: Outdated TLS ciphers on public gateway
findings.description: The public gateway negotiates deprecated TLS versions and weak cipher suites, falling below the bank's cryptographic baseline.

findings.title: CSRF on customer profile update
findings.description: The profile update endpoint does not validate an anti-CSRF token, allowing a malicious site to change a logged-in customer's details via a forged request.

findings.title: Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoint
findings.description: The authentication endpoint lacks rate limiting, enabling credential-stuffing and brute-force attempts against customer accounts.

findings.title: Verbose error messages leak stack traces
findings.description: Unhandled server errors return stack traces with internal file paths and framework versions to the client, aiding reconnaissance.

findings.title: Excessive session timeout on retail banking
findings.description: Retail banking sessions remain valid far longer than policy permits, widening the window for session hijacking on shared devices.

findings.title: Insufficient logging in transaction service
findings.description: The transaction service does not log security-relevant events consistently, hampering detection and investigation of incidents.

findings.title: Weak password complexity policy
findings.description: The identity platform enforces a weak password complexity policy, increasing the likelihood of guessable credentials.
